title: Duration/Timeslots for Calendar
excerpt: I want to use monday.com as a project management tool, BUT the calendar does not support time slots for events. It only allows an event for 1 hour but in real life this never fits. Either it is too short or too long. So …

posts.author: Geserit (Christopher Jeckl)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to use monday.com as a project management tool, BUT the calendar does not support time slots for events. It only allows an event for 1 hour but in real life this never fits. Either it is too short or too long. So whats the use exactly to implement a time functionality to the calendar and then not allow defining how long the event takes???
I saw that a couple of guys already requested this for years. Is it really such a difficult feature???

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Geserit! Thanks for posting.

Our team is aware of the issue and is looking into adding this for Q3 of this year. I can definitely understand how it’d be helpful to have time slots/duration on the calendar view.

Now we believe people use it for the beginning of meetings and for scheduling when to start projects, but it would be very useful if a duration could be set for those.

Stay on the look out!

posts.author: EVOCEAN (Philip Zollinger)
posts.cookedHtml: What is the status of this when is it coming?

posts.author: Viewport (Julius Jeppe)
posts.cookedHtml:
Very interested in this feature - we are now using Google Calendar to block out repeating monthly departmental tasks, but need this integrated into Monday so we can check the tasks off, leave notes etc.

Any update on when this is being rolled out?

posts.author: MirkoFortuna (Mirko)
posts.cookedHtml: How far along is this function to see the duration of an appointment on the calendar? Is it active?

posts.author: ChrisH (Chris Hartley)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this yet?

posts.author: adibar (adi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Would also like to get update on this feature please
It’s indeed necessary

posts.author: MissMonday (Claudia Wengle)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would really love an update on this.
I am currently switching all our main processes to monday, and I would like to automate e-mail templates that will give our customers date and time for each slot of our online webinars.

posts.author: kimmyM (Kimmy )
posts.cookedHtml: I would love to see this as well I currently work in events and would love to have a timeline of the show schedule

posts.author: KatieB (Katie Byrne)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update please @TRB-monday.com ? This is a fairly critical feature!
Thanks

posts.author: BjarkeBentzen (Bjarke Schou Bentzen)
posts.cookedHtml:
We really also need this feature.
We have a customer which we are building up Monday boards. Promised them this would be a feature end of 21 due to your answers here Monday support.
Could you at least give an update? @TRB-monday.com

posts.author: jptcc (Juliana P)
posts.cookedHtml: any update on this? we need this to plant for event/seminars. Thks

posts.author: BjarkeBentzen (Bjarke Schou Bentzen)
posts.cookedHtml:
any update on this Monday.
We have a client we promised this?

posts.author: Bill_In_Ohio (Bill Duncan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had thought, while looking at the Calendar Widget and example template, that this kind of functionality was built in now. With columns like date, time and duration, you would think that I would be able to use the Calendar View to be able to see meetings that are scheduled without having to leave monday.com. This isn’t at all useful however.

I understand that it isn’t a scheduling software, but if you are pushing the WorkOS model, then I should be able to incorporate my meetings into a workflow (they are still an essential part of work after all) and be able to generate a working calendar from your example. Sure, I can see all the items listed at the top of the day, but with the amount of information visible, it just appears as a big blob since it is only tied to the date, not the hour. Even without the item being tied to the duration, having them appear at the hour mark would be helpful.

I just deleted the view since I can’t use it at all.

It would be nice if I was able to display all meetings setup in a board, based on those criteria. If the view allows me to show the hours, I should be able to see that a meeting for project X is at 8:00 AM, a review of Project y’s tasks is happening at 10:30 AM, and project z’s close-out is at 2:30 PM, with them all at the correct “spots” on the calendar. Bonus points if I could tie the duration to “cover” the hours on the calendar, but I would settle for just having it tied to the time.

posts.author: MikaelZarb (Mikael Zarb)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m new to Monday.com and this is a must have feature for us! Do you have any updates as I’m seeing that this should have been implimented in Q3 last year…

posts.author: ANRes (Anya)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on when this will be included/changed for the calendar view?

posts.author: fureverafter (Michelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
And is there any way to change which time slots are shown, so that it only shows the business hours and not all 24 hours of the day?
It’s very frustrating having to scroll down every single time the view is opened because it starts at 12am.
And is there a way to make it “fit” the screen area that it’s in as well, so one can see the entire day at a glance?!

posts.author: idoputer (ido puterkovski)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this request! also for the time slots feature

posts.author: Raincloud (Anthony Adamson)
posts.cookedHtml: This is long overdue according to your earlier post. Looking for a solution only to end up in a dead thread is no fun.

posts.author: AndyISZ (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml: Just looking for ananswer for this, as it seems like a no-brainer to be able to put hours in a project plan, but seems like it’s not possible… This is a dealbreaker for us.

posts.author: Muhammad352 (Muhammad352)
posts.cookedHtml: The “Time Slot Duration” refers to the duration of a slot on the calendar. For example, if your Time Slot Duration is set to 15 minutes, your calendar will look like the below image. The appointment listed is a 15-minute appointment.

posts.author: MattHays (Matt Hays)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a custom calendar app out there but it costs a fortune - seems a lot for simply wanting to add a duration element.

Has anyone looked at developing their own and how difficult that might be?

posts.author: misler (Mitchell Isler)
posts.cookedHtml: Here to see if there has been any movement on this. It has been more than a year since @TRB-monday.com suggested this might be released. Even an update to say “no, it won’t be possible for a while” would be good. Any current workarounds for this are really clunky.

posts.author: Jessa227 (Jessica Jester)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for an update on this feature request. Any idea when this may be added?

posts.author: OfficeHounds (Maggie Langley)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. It’s a terrible user experience having all 24 hours displaying! It makes it too frustrating to use.

posts.author: tgrossutti (Thomas Grossutti)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello. I see this was supposedly going to be resolved Q3 2022. Any update on when we might see something on this?

posts.author: Deafzone (Tony Barlow)
posts.cookedHtml: That is only one thing letting down Monday - it got very weak calendar features. While the timeline column is good for project management - I want start and end time on the same day to create and be shown as a block on the calendar.

posts.author: RoseZ (rose.zandvliet@talpanetwork.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
@monday when is this getting released? Would lo the change the duration for specific events. Currently Monday is unusable as a resource planning and event planning tool.

Can you give an update?

posts.author: TonyaR (Tonya Rineer)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree. I’m currently exploring the software and this is a big deal breaker. Without this feature, the functionality of the calendar, on its own, is rendered useless for real life. This one feature would be a game changer for sure.

It was mentioned that it was considered as a focus back in Q3 of 2021. chance that’s coming in 2024?

posts.author: Cari (Cari A.)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday-team Do you have any update on adding this feature? We keep coming across different scenarios where having task duration by hours integrated in the Calendar view would be extremely beneficial for projects, work management, and daily scheduling.

posts.author: HebaGirgiss (Heba Girgiss)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s Q1 of 2024 and we are still without a feature that was supposed to be implemented in Q3 of 2021. This is such a basic functionality of any calendar. Is there any updated timeline on this?

posts.author: HebaGirgiss (Heba Girgiss)
posts.cookedHtml: I am likely going to switch to Notion. It’s free and there’s a learning curve of course, but it has most of the functionality I use Monday for.

posts.author: RomainD (Darson)
posts.cookedHtml: Any news ? Big deal breaker here…

posts.author: emmaceolin (Emma)
posts.cookedHtml: I have been using Monday.com for 4 years and waiting on this simple, necessary feature that allows for duration/timeslots for calendar (time blocking). Though I love Monday, this is a feature that I place high value on so I’ve just made the switch to Asana.

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, We’ve developed a new calendar app that enables you to transform items into dynamic calendar events with. check it out: monday.com apps marketplace

posts.author: Cari (Cari A.)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the suggestion @Dor_appSolution , however we don’t feel that this simple functionality should require us to pay an additional $100+/month. This feature should be baked into the monday software as it is a basic code that most other programs offer.

posts.author: markloring (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree. This should be baked into Monday.com.

posts.author: bdmur (Brittany)
posts.cookedHtml: Seriously. Why can we not simply change the calendar view to business hours only? I’m using this for social media management, and our team is obviously not posting at 1:00 am. This is a basic UX feature!

posts.author: bdmur (Brittany)
posts.cookedHtml: This isn’t a use case necessitating an add-on for more $$$. This is basic, daily business need and arguably one we’re already paying for.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
I hear we are supposed to get dates with durations this or next quarter. Not sure when that would also land in the calendar.

If it helps, event durations by way of an extra end date column are a standard, free feature in Calendar Plus.

posts.author: Fentor (Julian L)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also love such a (very basic) feature

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are definitely in need of the ability to have per event durations recorded and visible in the Calendar View within our organization. Feature requests calendar
Currently I am finding that a default duration can be set, but we need to be able to adjust per event (item).
@TRB-monday.com I see this was expected to be released Q3 2021, with no further updates from monday personnel I can see. Can we get a current status? Are there any workarounds available in monday itself?

posts.author: TheSleepingBarber (Ken)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wow time flies - any updates?

This is essential for the many people who time block their calendars.

posts.author: DipaliPatankar (Dipali Patankar)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature that was on the roadmap for Q3 2021?

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Geserit,

Choosing monday.com for project management is always a commendable decision. However, to generate time slots for multiple events, utilizing the intuitive Calendar View and Widget tool is advisable. With this tool, you can create and share event dates and schedule appointments with ease. This centralized tool enables you and your team/clients to see all upcoming events with greater transparency.

Our dedicated monday.com consultants can help you configure and set up this widget tool as per your requirements!

Connect with us today to use the calendar widget!

posts.author: markloring (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this Q3 2021 feature AND the feature is needed to support time blocking (adjustable start and end dates-times) of Items AND Subitems?

posts.author: Vincentlvl (Vincent de `kooker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, Is there any update on this feature? We are considering migrating to monday but this is a dealbreaker for us if not implemented. Not to pressure anyone but please let me know then we can decide to migrate or not.

thank you

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m anxious for this feature for our company also. Once in place it will be far more realistic for our Commercial team to use calendaring in monday CRM. In the meantime it really hampers the usefulness.

posts.author: bojo3811 (Chris B)
posts.cookedHtml: We are also looking for this feature. It is a little disappointing the request has been out there for so long with what seems like no progress.

posts.author: NikolaMastilovic (Nikola Mastilovic (SpicyTab))
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys!

Check out Better Calendar View - It sounds like it may help with this. Among other things, it allows for creation of items with variable duration - From 30 minutes to 30 years if you like!

posts.author: FerdinandoPH (Fernando Pérez Holguín)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m using the calendar widget, and I have noticed that, when switching to week/day view, all tasks appear as 30 minute tasks. I would like to be able to change the tasks’ duration, defining a start and end time

posts.author: markloring (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: This capability is critical for project management scheduling. The lack of it really limits Monday.com being a useful Project Management tool.

posts.author: Terry.Heard (Terry Heard)
posts.cookedHtml:
That is part of the body of work included in this which is already on the roadmap for development.

Start and end time in date column Feature requests
As soon as an start time is selected, and the column is in sync with your calendar it shows 1 hour in the calendar. This, in some cases can work wuite confusing, because this specific item (pulse) can represent a workshop of 3 hours. Being able to select an end time would solve that. [image]

posts.author: Dor_appSolution (Dor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @markloring @Terry.Heard @FerdinandoPH, We’ve developed a new calendar app that enables you to transform items into dynamic calendar events, including start and end times. check it out: monday.com apps marketplace

posts.author: markloring (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for the notification of the availability of MasterPlan Calendar. I see there is no Security & compliance information with regard to how the data that will be accessed (e.g. profile info of users, etc) will be accessed or protected by appSolution. What is that policy?
Best, Mark

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Terry.Heard @FerdinandoPH I also wanted to make you aware of another, free option available in the marketplace, Calendar Plus, that also supports event durations.

  • It’s free
  • Event durations for items and subitems – just drag and resize
  • It can display multiple dates and timelines per item
  • Display your team’s or customers’ public holidays to plan around them
  • Milestone markers
  • Did I say it’s free?

We don’t display any extra column information on events just yet.

@markloring Our privacy policy lays out what data goes where. We’re always available for specific questions; you can reach us via the email displayed at the bottom of that page.

posts.author: nicoleanne (Nicole)
posts.cookedHtml:
We want to view the calendar view on a weekly basis for project and task management on our end. In there we can see blocks of time the team is busy on tasks and see if they have spare time to work on other tasks for the week.

Thing though, the current calendar view only has a fixed event duration for all tasks, and cannot customise it to differ per task. One task takes 30 minutes to make, but another task takes 4 hours to complete.

If possible to have that flexibility in the duration in cthe calendar view so we can easily see the capacity of the team throughout the day with time allocations put into place, that would be amazing.

We can use other calendar apps for this, but Monday having the feature to see it in a board, change status, etc + calendar view would be perfect if this was possible.

Thanks!

posts.author: manallarte (Luca Bovenzi)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like to create an event calendar with the calendar view. Each event has a date and time and a specific duration. I noticed that the actual calendar view allows me to create only eevnts with a default duration. How can I change the duration and see it in the calendar view?

posts.author: jailynnlucidday (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @manallarte !

So unfortunately this is not a current feature within Monday.com. I believe that the default value is there regardless of the duration within the event.

Hope this brings clarity!

posts.author: manallarte (Luca Bovenzi)
posts.cookedHtml: And if this is true, what is the use of a calendar?

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml: For other people looking for this: there’s a feature request here you can vote on to raise the priority. In the meantime you can opt to use Calendar Plus to display a start and end date.
